sources.title: Intellijel MIDI 1U System Manual (covers CVx 1U) — Firmware 1.2, Rev 2026.01.06

name: CVx
description: 1U expander for Intellijel MIDI 1U. Adds 8 fully assignable CV/gate outputs via I2C. Chain up to four CVx for 32 outs and 8 extra synths (mono, poly, or drum). Jacks configured in Intellijel Config app.

controls.name: Expander ID DIP
controls.description: Two DIP switches on the back assign this CVx an ID from 1 to 4 when chaining multiple CVx units. Each CVx must have a unique ID.
controls.detail: SW1 down/SW2 down = Exp 1 · SW1 up/SW2 down = Exp 2 · SW1 down/SW2 up = Exp 3 · SW1 up/SW2 up = Exp 4

controls.name: Output 1–8 assignment
controls.description: Each of the 8 output jacks is independently configured via the Intellijel Config app: Output Type, source Synth (1–10), CC #, and CV polarity.
controls.detail: Output Type: Pitch · Gate · Gate (Any) · Trigger · Velocity · Aftertouch · CC · CC High-Res · Clock (Always/Running) · Reset · Run

outputs.name: Out 1
outputs.description: User-assignable CV/gate output. Type and source synth set in Intellijel Config app.

behaviors.name: Adds 8 MIDI synths
behaviors.description: Connecting one CVx raises MIDI 1U's synth count from 2 to 10. Synths 3–10 can be configured as monophonic, polyphonic, or drum (note-mapped).

behaviors.name: Polyphonic MIDI-to-CV
behaviors.trigger: MIDI note-on on a Poly synth channel
behaviors.description: When multiple outs on the same CVx (or chained CVx) are assigned to the same Poly synth, voices are allocated across those outs — up to 8-voice polyphony with per-voice Pitch/Gate/Velocity.

behaviors.name: Drum-note mapping
behaviors.trigger: MIDI note number on a Drum synth channel
behaviors.description: Each out on a Drum synth is bound to a specific MIDI note number and fires Gate, Trigger, or Velocity when that note arrives — for sample-kit trigger buses.

behaviors.name: Free CC / clock routing
behaviors.trigger: MIDI CC, clock, start/stop on a mapped synth
behaviors.description: Any out can be re-purposed as a CC CV (uni/bipolar, optional High-Res), Aftertouch CV, extra Clock (arbitrary division 1–192), Reset, or Run gate.

patchIdeas.name: 4-voice polyphonic MIDI synth
patchIdeas.patch: Assign Outs 1–4 as Pitch and Outs 5–8 as Gate of a single Poly synth to get 4-voice MIDI polyphony into four VCO voices.
steps: In Intellijel Config, Synths column, set Synth 3: MIDI Channel = 1, Type = Polyphonic.
Expander 1 column: Out 1×1 → Output Type = Pitch, Synth = 3.
Repeat for Out 1×2, 1×3, 1×4 → all Pitch, Synth 3 (this gives 4 voices).
Out 1×5 → Output Type = Gate, Synth = 3. Repeat for Out 1×6, 1×7, 1×8 (4 gates, one per voice).
Patch Out 1 → VCO A 1V/oct, Out 2 → VCO B 1V/oct, Out 3 → VCO C, Out 4 → VCO D.
Patch Out 5 → EG A trig, Out 6 → EG B trig, Out 7 → EG C, Out 8 → EG D (each EG drives its own VCA).
Play a 4-note chord on MIDI channel 1 from your DAW or keyboard.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Outs 1–4 — four independent 1V/oct pitch CVs, each ±5V. Outs 5–8 — four gates, 0–5V.
patchIdeas.hear: A true 4-voice polyphonic patch: each key press lights up one voice (round-robin), chords sustain across all four VCOs with independent envelopes.
patchIdeas.diagram:
CVx:
* Out 1-4 = Pitch (Poly Synth 3) · Out 5-8 = Gate (Poly Synth 3)

- CVx (Out 1) p> VCO A (V/Oct)
- CVx (Out 2) p> VCO B (V/Oct)
- CVx (Out 3) p> VCO C (V/Oct)
- CVx (Out 4) p> VCO D (V/Oct)
- CVx (Out 5) g> EG A (Trig)
- CVx (Out 6) g> EG B (Trig)
- CVx (Out 7) g> EG C (Trig)
- CVx (Out 8) g> EG D (Trig) // 4-voice MIDI polyphony

patchIdeas.name: Paraphonic chord with Gate (Any)
patchIdeas.patch: Four pitch outs share a single envelope via Gate (Any) on Out 5 — cheap paraphonic chords through one VCA.
steps: Synths column: Synth 4 = Polyphonic on MIDI Channel 2.
Expander column: Out 1×1 to Out 1×4 → Output Type = Pitch, Synth = 4.
Out 1×5 → Output Type = Gate (Any), Synth = 4 (fires whenever ANY voice is held).
Patch Out 1 → VCO 1, Out 2 → VCO 2, Out 3 → VCO 3, Out 4 → VCO 4 (all into a common mixer).
Patch Out 5 → envelope trigger; envelope → single VCA CV on the mixed VCO sum.
Send a chord on MIDI channel 2 — all four notes sound together through one envelope.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Out 5 — one master gate 0–5V that stays high while any voice is held.
patchIdeas.hear: A full chord opens and closes with one shared envelope shape (paraphonic). Cheaper on envelopes/VCAs than true poly but locked to a single amplitude shape.
patchIdeas.diagram:
CVx:
* Out 1-4 = Pitch (Poly Synth 4) · Out 5 = Gate (Any)

- CVx (Out 1) p> VCO 1 (V/Oct)
- CVx (Out 2) p> VCO 2 (V/Oct)
- CVx (Out 3) p> VCO 3 (V/Oct)
- CVx (Out 4) p> VCO 4 (V/Oct)
- VCO 1-4 (Out) -> Mixer (In) -> VCA (In)
- CVx (Out 5) g> EG (Trig)
- EG (Out) >> VCA (CV) // shared paraphonic envelope

patchIdeas.name: MIDI drum bus
patchIdeas.patch: Map 8 MIDI note numbers to 8 trigger outs for a drum rack driving individual sample modules.
steps: Synths column: Synth 5 = Drum on MIDI Channel 10 (the GM drum channel).
Expander: Out 1×1 → Output Type = Trigger, Synth = 5, MIDI Note = C1 (kick).
Out 1×2 → Trigger, Synth 5, MIDI Note = D1 (snare).
Out 1×3 → Trigger, Synth 5, MIDI Note = F#1 (closed hat). Repeat 1×4–1×8 for other drum notes.
Patch each Out to its drum/sample module's trigger input (Out 1 → kick, Out 2 → snare, etc.).
Play the mapped drum notes on channel 10 from a DAW drum track — each note fires its own jack.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Out 1–8 — eight 5V/5ms trigger pulses, each firing on its mapped MIDI note number.
patchIdeas.hear: A full MIDI drum rack in the modular: kick on C1, snare on D1, hat on F#1, etc. Velocity can be added by assigning another CVx out as Velocity on the same note.
patchIdeas.diagram:
CVx:
* Synth 5 = Drum Ch10 · Out 1=Trig C1 · Out 2=Trig D1 · Out 3=Trig F#1 ... Out 8=Trig

- CVx (Out 1) t> Kick (Trig)
- CVx (Out 2) t> Snare (Trig)
- CVx (Out 3) t> Hat (Trig) // per-note drum bus

patchIdeas.name: Mod wheel + aftertouch expression bus
patchIdeas.patch: Free up Outs 1–2 as two expressive CC CVs for filter and VCA colour, while keeping pitch/gate on the MIDI 1U.
steps: Keep Synth 1 (MIDI 1U) as Monophonic on channel 1 — Pitch/Gate/Vel come off the MIDI 1U panel as usual.
Expander: Out 1×1 → Output Type = CC, CC Number = 1 (Mod Wheel), Synth = 1, CV Polarity = Unipolar 0–5V.
Out 1×2 → Output Type = Pressure (Aftertouch), Synth = 1.
Patch MIDI 1U Pitch → VCO 1V/oct, MIDI 1U Gate → EG trig.
Patch CVx Out 1 → VCF cutoff CV (mod wheel opens the filter).
Patch CVx Out 2 → VCA CV or a second VCF input (aftertouch adds emphasis when you press keys harder).
Play — mod wheel sweeps the filter, pressing harder on a controller with aftertouch adds colour.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Out 1 — mod wheel CV 0–5V into VCF. Out 2 — channel aftertouch CV 0–5V into VCA/VCF.
patchIdeas.hear: Expressive playing: mod wheel opens the filter, leaning into a key adds pressure-based modulation. Pitch and gate still come from MIDI 1U.
patchIdeas.diagram:
CVx:
* Out 1 = CC1 Mod Wheel (Synth 1) · Out 2 = Aftertouch (Synth 1)

- MIDI 1U (Pitch) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- MIDI 1U (Gate) g> EG (Trig)
- CVx (Out 1) >> VCF (Cutoff) // mod wheel sweep
- CVx (Out 2) >> VCA (CV) // aftertouch swell

patchIdeas.name: Extra clocks at different divisions
patchIdeas.patch: Three CVx outs become three independently divided clocks for polymetric sequencer sync.
steps: Expander: Out 1×1 → Output Type = Clock (Always), Clock Division = 24 (quarter notes).
Out 1×2 → Clock (Always), Clock Division = 6 (16th notes).
Out 1×3 → Clock (Always), Clock Division = 18 (dotted 8ths — useful for polymeter).
Out 1×4 → Output Type = Reset (fires on MIDI Start).
Patch Out 1 → Sequencer A clock; Out 2 → Sequencer B clock; Out 3 → Sequencer C clock.
Patch Out 4 → all sequencers' Reset inputs (mult if needed).
Start playback in your DAW.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Out 1 — 1/4 note clock, Out 2 — 1/16 note clock, Out 3 — dotted-8th clock (all 0–5V pulses). Out 4 — reset pulse on Start.
patchIdeas.hear: Three sequencers run in time with the DAW but at different rates, creating a polyrhythmic interplay that all restart together when you press play.
patchIdeas.diagram:
CVx:
* Out 1 = Clock /24 · Out 2 = Clock /6 · Out 3 = Clock /18 · Out 4 = Reset

- CVx (Out 1) c> Seq A (Clock)
- CVx (Out 2) c> Seq B (Clock)
- CVx (Out 3) c> Seq C (Clock)
- CVx (Out 4) t> Seq A/B/C (Reset) // polymetric DAW sync
